spec_match.rationale: `beforeinput` handler reads `dataTransfer.items`, calls `preventDefault()`, and routes each file into a side gallery — the exact pattern an IME-aware editor would use.

interactive.rationale: Editable surface, four simulated-insertion buttons (GIF/PNG/WebP/JPEG), a real Android keyboard path for on-device users, and a gallery that updates live.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises lift-to-gallery on insertion and toolbar buttons for desktop simulation — both work.

edge_cases.rationale: Single-item flow only; no bulk insertion, no rejection branch, and no demonstration of what happens when the user inserts the same file twice (the gallery should dedupe or at least mark it).

references.rationale: No chromestatus.com/feature/5484282563919872 link anywhere on the page.

design_system.rationale: Page CSS uses variables, but the simulation code embeds 15 raw hex colours in the canvas gradient palette. They are content-not-chrome, so the rule is debatable — flagging as `partial` because the rule says `never raw hex` without an exception. Consider documenting the exception or moving to CSS custom properties read via getComputedStyle.

openQuestions.title: Add chromestatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: The see-also section has no link to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5484282563919872. Add it so the slug-recovery script keeps working.

openQuestions.title: Bulk-insert behaviour
openQuestions.detail: Stickers can arrive in sets. Add a `simulate sticker pack` button that fires three items in a single dataTransfer so the gallery loop handles `items.length > 1`.

openQuestions.title: Reject path for unsupported MIME
openQuestions.detail: The handler currently accepts any image/*. Add a toggle that gates a type set, and show what the gallery does when a rejected item arrives (banner / silent drop with log).

openQuestions.title: Document the raw-hex palette
openQuestions.detail: The 15 hex constants drive the canvas-generated thumbnails for visual variety. Either add a comment that calls them out as content (not chrome) — or read the colours from CSS variables via `getComputedStyle(document.documentElement)`.

openQuestions.title: Dedupe identical inserts
openQuestions.detail: Inserting the same sticker twice currently produces two gallery items. Hash on `file.name + file.size` and at least flag duplicates — that is the pattern most apps want.

summary: Good UX shape (editor + side gallery) but missing the chromestatus reference link and limited to single-item insertions. The raw hex palette in JS is functional but worth either documenting or moving into variables.
